question_text:
Sliding Window Maximum

You are given an array of integers nums, there is a sliding window of size k which is moving from the very left of the array to the very right. You can only see the k numbers in the window. Each time the sliding window moves right by one position, return the maximum of each window.

Constraints:

  • 1 <= nums.length <= 105
  • -104 <= nums[i] <= 104
  • 1 <= k <= nums.length
input_format: An integer array nums and a window size k.
output_format: An array of maximums, one per sliding window position.
explanation:
Monotonic Deque

Maintain a deque of indices in decreasing order of their values. For each index i: remove indices from the back that have smaller values (maintain decreasing order). Remove the front index if it is out of the current window (i - dq[0] >= k). Append the current index. After filling the first window, the front of the deque is the max.

Time: O(N). Space: O(K).
